title: SSM documents should have the block public sharing setting enabled
description: This control checks whether the block public sharing setting is enabled for AWS Systems Manager documents. The control fails if the block public sharing setting is disabled for Systems Manager documents.
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether the block public sharing setting is enabled for AWS Systems Manager documents. The control fails if the block public sharing setting is disabled for Systems Manager documents.
what_the_fix_does: Disables public sharing for SSM documents to reduce unauthorized exposure.

findings.title: SSM documents should have the block public sharing setting enabled

recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_high__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'exception_review'. Risk is high and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.00, privilege_level=1.00, internet_exposure=0.80, matched_signals=none. Relationship context is incomplete, so the recommendation remains conservative and advisory unless policy overrides it.

execution_guidance.label: Disable public SSM document sharing
execution_guidance.blast_radius_summary: Affects account-wide security posture for account 696505809372.

pre_checks.message: Confirm this change is intended for AWS::::Account:696505809372 in account 696505809372 (us-east-1).
Confirm the repository, deployment path, and approver that own this infrastructure change before generating the PR bundle.
Publicly shared SSM document consumers may lose access after enforcement.

execution_guidance.expected_outcome: PR mode produces a reviewable infrastructure change: Public sharing of SSM documents will be blocked. After merge and apply through your IaC path, Security Hub should reflect the remediated state within 12-24 hours.

post_checks.message: Review the generated bundle or diff, then capture plan and apply evidence after the change is merged through the normal infrastructure workflow.
Verify the completed change only affected the intended account scope for AWS::::Account:696505809372.
Trigger immediate re-evaluation after the change lands and confirm the action closes without reopening.

rollback.summary: Use this rollback path to revert the infrastructure change through the same reviewed delivery workflow.
notes: Prefer reverting through version control and your normal deployment pipeline so rollback stays auditable.
